The '''''Dragon Pearl''''' was [[Jiliac Desilijic Tiron|Jiliac the]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] personal starship.

Originating as a heavily-armed [[Minstrel-class space yacht/Legends|''Minstrel''-class space yacht]], the ''Dragon Pearl'' had undergone modifications to be able to hold six [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] and two [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]]. Unlike her nephew [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]], Jiliac was very diligent about keeping her yacht combat-ready, and promptly replaced any lost [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].

Commandeered by [[Mako Spince]], the ''Dragon Pearl'' would serve as the smuggler fleet's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] in the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|defense]] of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] in [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]]. It was commanded by Mako and piloted by [[Ana Blue]] in the battle. The yacht sustained very heavy damage when it fought the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruiser]] [[Renthal's Vigilance|''Vigilance'']] in the opening stages of the battle, but managed to batter down the larger ship's shields so that [[Drea Renthal]]'s [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] ships ''[[Minestra]]'' and ''[[Too Late Now]]'' could move in and crippled the cruiser. Later in the battle, the ''Dragon Pearl'' and a single [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighter]] managed to cripple the [[Neutron Star-class bulk cruiser/Legends|''Neutron Star''-class bulk cruiser]] [[Liquidator (Neutron Star-class)|''Liquidator'']] in a surprise attack from behind. Following the battle, Mako and Ana spent some time in hiding on [[Smuggler's Run/Legends|Smuggler's Run]], in case Jiliac was angered by the damage to her yacht.

However, Jiliac was so obsessed with motherhood that she did not even notice that her beloved ship had been damaged. Attempting to get Jiliac out of a maternal slump, Jabba the Hutt later had the ship restored to operational status.
